title: All Primes in Range

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all prime numbers in the range [LLL, RRR].


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains two space separated integers, LLL and RRR - The start and end of the range.


Output Format

Print on a single line, all prime numbers between LLL and RRR (inclusive) in increasing order with a space between them.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space-separated integers L and R denoting the start and end of the range (inclusive).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print all prime numbers between L and R (inclusive) in increasing order on a single line, separated by a single space. If there are no primes in the range, print an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. The primes between 2 and 11 inclusive are printed.
Only one test case. Both 11 and 13 are prime.
The range consists of a single number 5, which is prime.

solution.approach: Pre‑compute primality for every integer up to the maximum possible R (1000) using the Sieve of Eratosthenes. The sieve runs once in O(MAX log log MAX) time. For each test case we simply iterate from L to R and output the numbers marked as prime. This yields O(R‑L+1) per test case, which is trivial for the given limits.
solution.timeComplexity: Precomputation: O(MAX log log MAX) where MAX = 1000. Per test case: O(R‑L+1). Overall O(T * MAX) in the worst case, well within limits.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(MAX) for the boolean array storing primality (≈1001 booleans).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st;int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());final int MAX=1000;boolean[] isPrime=new boolean[MAX+1];Arrays.fill(isPrime,true);isPrime[0]=isPrime[1]=false;for(int p=2;p*p<=MAX;p++)if(isPrime[p])for(int q=p*p;q<=MAX;q+=p)isPrime[q]=false;StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int L=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int R=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());boolean first=true;for(int i=L;i<=R;i++)if(isPrime[i]){if(!first)sb.append(' ');sb.append(i);first=false;}sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
